{

Let me check your TOOLS.md for calendar info:

# TOOLS.md - Local Notes

Skills define _how_ tools work. This file is for _your_ specifics — the stuff that's unique to your setup.

## What Goes Here

Things like:

- Camera names and locations
- SSH hosts and aliases
- Preferred voices for TTS
- Speaker/room names
- Device nicknames
- Anything environment-specific

## Examples

```markdown
### Cameras

- living-room → Main area, 180° wide angle
- front-door → Entrance, motion-triggered

### SSH

- home-server → 192.168.1.100, user: admin

### TTS

- Preferred voice: "Nova" (warm, slightly British)
- Default speaker: Kitchen HomePod
```

## Why Separate?

Skills are shared. Your setup is yours. Keeping them apart means you can update skills without losing your notes, and share skills without leaking your infrastructure.

---

Add whatever helps you do your job. This is your cheat sheet.
Let me try a few different approaches to access your calendar:

And make sure **Terminal** (or whatever shell/terminal app you're using) has access enabled?

Once that's granted, I should be able to pull your schedule. The calendar data exists - icalBuddy just can't read it without permission.

arguments.command:
osascript -e '
tell application "Calendar"
    set startDate to current date
    set endDate to startDate + (7 * days)
    set eventList to {}
    repeat with cal in calendars
        set calEvents to (every event of cal whose start date ≥ startDate and start date ≤ endDate)
        repeat with evt in calEvents
            set end of eventList to (summary of evt & " - " & (start date of evt as string))
        end repeat
    end repeat
    return eventList
end tell' 2>&1

content.text: The AppleScript is taking too long. Let me try a simpler approach:

arguments.command:
osascript -e '
tell application "Calendar"
    set todayDate to date (short date string of (current date))
    set eventList to {}
    repeat with cal in calendars
        try
            set calEvents to (every event of cal whose start date ≥ todayDate)
            repeat with evt in calEvents
                set end of eventList to (summary of evt & " | " & start date of evt)
            end repeat
        end try
    end repeat
    if length of eventList > 10 then
        set eventList to items 1 thru 10 of eventList
    end if
    return eventList
end tell' 2>&1

content.text: Let me try using a more direct AppleScript approach with a specific calendar:

arguments.command:
osascript -e '
tell application "Calendar"
    activate
    set output to ""
    set targetCal to calendar "Calendar"
    set evtList to events of targetCal
    repeat with i from 1 to 5
        try
            set evt to item i of evtList
            set output to output & (summary of evt) & " - " & (start date of evt) & "\n"
        end try
    end repeat
    return output
end tell' 2>&1

content.text: Let me check the Family calendar for more events:

arguments.command:
osascript -e '
tell application "Calendar"
    set output to ""
    set targetCal to calendar "Family"
    set evtList to events of targetCal
    repeat with i from 1 to 10
        try
            set evt to item i of evtList
            set output to output & (summary of evt) & " - " & (start date of evt) & "\n"
        end try
    end repeat
    return output
end tell' 2>&1

content.text: I see the node service isn't installed. The calendar access through AppleScript on the host is working though. Let me try to get a cleaner view of your upcoming events:

arguments.command:
osascript -e '
tell application "Calendar"
    set output to "📅 UPCOMING EVENTS THIS WEEK:\n\n"
    
    -- Check main Calendar
    try
        set mainCal to calendar "Calendar"
        set mainEvents to events of mainCal
        repeat with evt in mainEvents
            set evtDate to start date of evt
            set nowDate to current date
            set daysDiff to (evtDate - nowDate) / days
            if daysDiff ≥ 0 and daysDiff ≤ 7 then
                set output to output & "• " & (summary of evt) & " - " & (short date string of evtDate) & " at " & time string of evtDate & "\n"
            end if
        end repeat
    end try
    
    return output
end tell' 2>&1

arguments.old_string:
# TOOLS.md - Local Notes

Skills define _how_ tools work. This file is for _your_ specifics — the stuff that's unique to your setup.

## What Goes Here

Things like:

- Camera names and locations
- SSH hosts and aliases
- Preferred voices for TTS
- Speaker/room names
- Device nicknames
- Anything environment-specific

## Examples

```markdown
### Cameras

- living-room → Main area, 180° wide angle
- front-door → Entrance, motion-triggered

### SSH

- home-server → 192.168.1.100, user: admin

### TTS

- Preferred voice: "Nova" (warm, slightly British)
- Default speaker: Kitchen HomePod
```

## Why Separate?

Skills are shared. Your setup is yours. Keeping them apart means you can update skills without losing your notes, and share skills without leaking your infrastructure.

---

Add whatever helps you do your job. This is your cheat sheet.

Got it! I'll add "Highschool Blast" to your calendar every Saturday at 2 PM, with a reminder 2 hours before (at 12 PM).

arguments.command:
osascript -e '
tell application "Calendar"
    set targetCal to calendar "ianjames.ormo@gmail.com"
    
    -- Create the first event for this Saturday (March 29, 2026)
    set startDate to date "Saturday, March 29, 2026 at 2:00:00 PM"
    set endDate to startDate + (1 * hours)
    
    set newEvent to make new event at end of events of targetCal with properties {summary:"Highschool Blast", start date:startDate, end date:endDate}
    
    -- Add alarm for 2 hours before
    make new display alarm at end of display alarms of newEvent with properties {trigger interval:-120}
    
    return "Highschool Blast added for Saturday, March 29 at 2:00 PM with 2-hour reminder"
end tell' 2>&1

content.text: Highschool Blast added for Saturday, March 29 at 2:00 PM with 2-hour reminder

details.aggregated: Highschool Blast added for Saturday, March 29 at 2:00 PM with 2-hour reminder

content.text: Now let me set up the recurring weekly event and the 2-hour reminder notification:
Now let me also add the recurring event to your calendar for future Saturdays:

arguments.command:
osascript -e '
tell application "Calendar"
    set targetCal to calendar "ianjames.ormo@gmail.com"
    
    -- Create a few more recurring instances (next 4 Saturdays)
    repeat with i from 1 to 4
        set startDate to date "Saturday, March 29, 2026 at 2:00:00 PM"
        set startDate to startDate + (i * 7 * days)
        set endDate to startDate + (1 * hours)
        
        set newEvent to make new event at end of events of targetCal with properties {summary:"Highschool Blast", start date:startDate, end date:endDate}
        
        -- Add alarm for 2 hours before
        make new display alarm at end of display alarms of newEvent with properties {trigger interval:-120}
    end repeat
    
    return "Added Highschool Blast for the next 4 Saturdays with 2-hour reminders"
end tell' 2>&1

content.text: Added Highschool Blast for the next 4 Saturdays with 2-hour reminders

arguments.command:
osascript -e '
tell application "Calendar"
    set output to "📅 Ian'"'"'s Schedule - Friday, March 27, 2026:\n\n"
    set foundEvents to false
    
    -- Check all calendars for Friday events
    repeat with cal in calendars
        try
            set calEvents to events of cal
            repeat with evt in calEvents
                set evtDate to start date of evt
                set dayOfWeek to weekday of evtDate as string
                set dateString to short date string of evtDate
                
                -- Check if it'"'"'s Friday March 27, 2026
                if dateString contains "3/27/26" or dateString contains "27/3/26" then
                    set foundEvents to true
                    set output to output & "• " & (summary of evt) & " - " & (time string of evtDate) & " (" & (name of cal) & ")\n"
                end if
            end repeat
        end try
    end repeat
    
    if not foundEvents then
        set output to output & "No events found for Friday."
    end if
    
    return output
end tell' 2>&1

content.text: Let me try a simpler approach:

arguments.command:
osascript -e '
tell application "Calendar"
    set output to ""
    set targetCal to calendar "Calendar"
    set evtList to events of targetCal
    repeat with evt in evtList
        set evtDate to start date of evt
        if short date string of evtDate contains "3/27" then
            set output to output & "• " & (summary of evt) & " - " & (time string of evtDate) & "\n"
        end if
    end repeat
    return output
end tell' 2>&1
